title: Rebuild 20 You Can Not Be Serious
display_title: Rebuild 20 You Can Not Be Serious
wikitext:
[]: Rebuild 2.0: You Can (Not) Be Serious             Quote Post by Damaijin » Sat Jun 01, 2013 9:03 pm First off, I loved the film, so I'm not bashing here. I'm just poking fun. As we all know, Evangelion 2.0 is when the films go off the rails, first gradually, then full-blown "WTF?". I liked most of the changes made, especially the aquarium scene, the underlying theme of cooking, and an Asuka so different from the series that she wasn't even Asuka at all. There were some things that were a bit weird for me, though. 1. Fan Service + 14-year-old girls = Child Pornography -Anno, you perv. What's up with all the fan service? There's nothing wrong with fan service in general, but when 90% of it is being provided by adolescent girls, you're breaching into some creepy and unsettling territory. I don't even get anything out of female fan service, so it's not like I enjoyed it, but I still felt really, really uncomfortable just seeing it happen. 2. Some of the Angel Redesigns - The 3rd Angel was weird, but the part where it used its halo to get to the surface was just beautiful, so I didn't mind it. (Yes, I just called a bouncing cartoon bone corpse beautiful, but it was mostly because of the music that was playing.) I thought Clockiel was just a desperate attempt to do something so different from the series that it wound up looking tacky. Both it and the redesigned Sahaquiel were major CGI overkill. I really liked Zeruel's design from the series, so I wasn't overly pleased with its new version, either. The unravelling part was cool (again, thanks to the music), but everything after that was just "meh". Then when it grew tits, I was like, "Okay, now that's just ridiculous." 3. You can't fix a plot hole with another plot hole. - I appreciate how they tried to fix the Bardiel battle plot hole (the one where instead of having 3 Evas gang up on one Angel, they had the Evas spread out so they could get their asses kicked one by one) by having 01 being the only functioning Eva at the time and giving Bardiel extra arms. But this just sets up a plot hole of its own because it made Shinji's tantrum after the fight completely baseless. In the series, he didn't know the world would end if an Angel reached the compound until Kaji told him after the fight, and that's what brought him back to piloting. In the film continuity, Misato told him about this before the big fight with Ramiel, so he should have understood the consequences of his inaction, even if he didn't like it. I realize that this might not be a plot hole so much as it is that Shinji really is a selfish little bastard. (I will say I the tantrum itself was satisfying in its own way. I liked the impact that came from hearing Shinji berate himself for trusting his father. Also, it was totally cool to see an Eva used for something like that. It reminded me of Misato's comment, "Ooh, with four Evas, you could conquer the world!") 4. Uh, did you just give Zeruel nipples? - At least it wasn't a 14-year-old girl. (sees naked Rei, Rei's nipples inside Zeruel's core) Never mind. 5. The whole "Ayanami o kaesu" moment came out of nowhere. - Seriously, he watched her get eaten and didn't say a damn word, and didn't even mention her name until that point. Now all of a sudden, his only reason for fighting is just to save Rei, and his conviction for that is so powerful he can awaken a dead Eva by sheer force of will and send it into full-on god-mode? It seemed horribly contrived to fit in with the "We were right, those two could awaken the Eva" thing. I couldn't connect the two things myself, which ties in with, and brings me to, my next point... 6. Is there a god-mode section in the How to Pilot Your Evangelion Instruction Manual? - Apparently not, seeing as how Ritsuko had no idea what was going on and she practically wrote the damn thing. So, how could Shinji have known it was even possible to save Rei using the Eva? How could he have known she was taken into Zeruel's core and not just its digestive tract? How could he have known his A.T. Field had such functionality, or that the Evas had eye beams? And how'd he know how to use either? And how could 01 attain godhood when it's basically the same as an angel? I know this is reading too much into it, but I'm not really complaining. The curb-stomp battle between 01 and Zeruel probably ranks as one of my favorite scenes in all of anime. I didn't start asking those questions until much, much later, and they don't take anything away from the quality of the film as far as I'm concerned. 7. Tsubasa wo Kudasai - Playing a cutsie song during a savage dismemberment/evisceration is one thing. Playing another one during the ascension scene 20 minutes later is just cheap. I don't really have a reason for posting this. If you wanna comment on something I said or answer any of the questions, that's fine. Just use the corresponding numbers so I know what you're talking about. Otherwise, what bothered you about Rebuild 2.0? Remember, it's not bashing, it's poking fun.

[]:             Quote Post by Rei IV » Sat Jun 01, 2013 9:37 pm I think this particular topic belongs in the Rebuild section of the forums. You'll probably get responses there.

[]: Re: Rebuild 2.0: You Can (Not) Be Serious             Quote Post by Fuuforufu » Sat Jun 01, 2013 10:42 pm Damaijin wrote: 1. Fan Service + 14-year-old girls = Child Pornography

[]:             Quote Post by Jurrasic » Sun Jun 02, 2013 12:34 am I don't like people who rip into others for a few grammar errors or misspelled words, but bro...USE A SPELL-CHECK on your posts, PLEASE. Your spelling is so bad as to draw attention away from the content of your posts. Although I think I might want to sig the nabucosador key

[]: Re: Rebuild 2.0: You Can (Not) Be Serious             Quote Post by Stryker » Sun Jun 02, 2013 1:04 pm Fuuforufu wrote: When Zeruel ate the eva 00 and Rei, Mary says something like " he is assimilating the eva's body, everything with pilot included"

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Sun Jun 02, 2013 1:09 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Also, how could you possibly have a problem with Tsubasa wo Kudasai ? I mean, honestly.

[]:             Quote Post by Jinroh » Sun Jun 02, 2013 5:06 pm Someone complaining about Zeruel's boobs?! jeez

[]:             Quote Post by airman4 » Sun Jun 02, 2013 7:55 pm Mummy ! Rebuild is not as good as NGE! before it was better *ouahhh ouahhh cries everywhere* Why anno did you do that , nge is so much better in every point we need to go back to the past!!

[]:             Quote Post by Psychodrake » Mon Jun 03, 2013 9:46 am Honestly OP, I'll admit I hate fanservice, but the rest of your points just make it seem like you want NGE adapted in movie form. What's the point? NGE already exists. It's not going anywhere, it has done what it was created to do. Why do you want the same thing again? Rebuild is telling the same story with the same characters with a twist. It has been created to revive and revitalize the Evangelion brand, allowing new viewers to enjoy the series as we did. It allows the artists more freedom to go back and really experiment, see what boundaries they can push and it allows some of the most unique and interesting art assets out there to go back into play. And like someone else said, Zeruel's body was disturbing. Have you ever done art class before? Life drawing? Maybe you're just a bit sensitive to boobs, but it's a pretty natural thing and not always there to be a turn-on. Evangelion is a highly surrealist piece of work, so it's just natural to use the human body in these ways to invoke specific feelings.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Jun 03, 2013 12:52 pm I'm honestly not sure if the OP is trolling or not. I mean, complaining about fanservice-y 14 year olds when it was incredibly abundant in the original series? Complaining about an Eva being berserk not being in the instruction manual? Complaining about inappropriate songs playing during horrific, dissonant scenes? It's like he didn't watch the original anime...or is screwing with people.
